x-enumeration-literals.name: Pledge Collateral Agreement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Borrower keeps ownership, but possession/control is given to the lender or a custodian.
Used for: Financial assets (cash, securities).

Comment:
Example: Shares held in a blocked account controlled by the bank.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Mortgage Collateral Agreement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Borrower keeps ownership and possession, while the lender has a registered legal claim.
Used for: Real estate (houses, land).

Comment:
Example: A home loan secured by a property mortgage.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Lien Collateral Agreement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Lender has a right to retain or claim an asset until debt is repaid.
Used for: Deposits, accounts, or goods.

Comment:
Example: Bank lien on a savings account securing an overdraft.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Hypothecation Collateral Agreement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Borrower retains ownership and possession, lender has a charge without control.
Used for: Movable assets (vehicles, inventory).

Comment:
Example: Car loan where the borrower uses the car but it secures the loan.
